name: Rezayi et al. Formative Communication-Skills Synthetic Training Extract
population_or_context: Formative assessment tool for learner physicians practicing communication skills through simulated patient interactions across 8 clinical scenarios.
sample_size: The synthetic-data experiment reports 50 generated examples per case-learning-point pair and compares synthetic-only, limited real-only, and hybrid real-plus-synthetic training conditions.
methods: Fine-tuned DeBERTa/ACTA scoring models were evaluated with 5-fold cross-validation at the case level. Synthetic learner responses were generated for sparse learning points and evaluated through downstream scoring performance on real learner responses.
education_context: Medical education; learner physician communication-skills assessment using transcribed verbal responses and learning-point labels.
response_origin: Real learner responses plus Qwen-generated synthetic learner responses for learning points with sparse annotations.
rubric_label_space: Binary detection of whether each response demonstrates target communication learning points.
label_source: Real learner responses use human annotations and adjudication; synthetic responses are generated to demonstrate target learning points and evaluated through scorer performance on real annotated responses.
generation_model: Qwen2.5-32B-instruct / Qwen32B-4bit as reported in the paper.
generation_method: Prompt the LLM with task-specific instructions, target learning-point definitions, and selected positive examples from real learner data to generate realistic responses demonstrating the target learning point.
prompt_strategy: Task instructions, target learning-point definitions, and selected positive examples from real learner data.
evaluation_method: 5-fold cross-validation at the case level and binary F1 on real learner responses; ethical discussion distinguishes formative use from summative hybrid human-review use.
scorer_use: Synthetic responses support training augmentation for automated communication-skills scoring; real learner responses remain necessary for robust evaluation.
real_response_comparator: Performance is evaluated on real learner responses from unseen clinical scenarios; hybrid real-plus-synthetic training is compared with real-only and synthetic-only conditions.
reported_metrics: Mean binary F1 across 8 clinical scenarios is 0.94 for the broader scoring model; Table 3 reports average F1 of 0.757 for 50 synthetic examples versus 0.723 baseline, and 0.878 for 15 real plus 50 synthetic versus 0.793 for 15 real only.
limitations: The primary tool context is formative rather than high-stakes operational scoring.
The paper states synthetic data cannot fully replace real annotated learner responses for robust evaluation.
In summative contexts, the paper frames the models as hybrid systems where borderline or below-passing responses are reviewed by human raters.
The study is limited to 8 clinical scenarios and communication learning points.
